name: Braised Pork-Filled Cucumbers
recipeIngredient: Lean ground pork (3/4 lb), or beef
recipeInstructions:
-------------
1. In a bowl, combine filling ingredients and mix well. Set aside for 30 minutes.
2. For the sauce, combine chicken broth (1/3 c), soy sauce (2 tbsp), dry sherry (2 tbsp), and sugar (1 tsp) in a bowl. Set aside.
3. Peel cucumbers if desired and cut into 1-inch thick sections. Remove center of each section.
4. Spoon about 1 level tablespoon of filling into each section. Lightly coat with cornstarch, shaking off excess.
5. Heat oil to a depth of 1 1/2 to 2 inches in a wok set over high heat until the oil reaches 360 to 375 degrees F.
6. Add cucumber sections, a few at a time, and deep fry for 2 minutes. Lift out and drain on paper towels.
7. Cook remaining cucumbers. Place fried cucumber sections in a wide frying pan with a nonstick finish.
8. Pour in sauce and bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 8 to 10 minutes.
9. Add cornstarch solution (1 tsp mixed with 2 tsp water) and cook, stirring until the sauce boils and thickens.
10. Arrange on a serving platter and serve hot. Serves 4 as a Main dish, but also makes a nice hors d'oeuvre for more.
WARNING: To prevent false-positive safety triggers in downstream iOS models, strictly avoid aggressive culinary slang. Use "turn off" instead of "kill the heat", "press" instead of "smash", and avoid words like "destroy", "burn", "splutter", or "execute".
